rows.description: Computus Math: Hole. Rectoriq a Secunda Die Novembris 1724
rows.notes: Top title line. Handwriting partly faded and some letters abbreviated (e.g. 'Rectoriq'). Capital words visible but several letters uncertain (e.g. 'Math/Mat.' and 'Hole').
Date phrase readable though the script is slightly compressed; 'Decembris 1725' is clear, 'ad eundem diem' is legible albeit in old-style script.
Section heading (looks like 'Arrearage' / 'Arrearige'). Clear as a structural label with no amounts in the row itself.
Pounds/shillings/pence columns are well aligned for this first monetary row. '60 0 0' is clear and passes currency validation.

Pounds '58' and shillings '3' are legible. The rightmost pence area shows a small mark that could be '2' with an extraneous flourish; original looks like '29' but 29 pence invalid, so interpreted as 2d based on alignment with pence column. Significant ambiguity remains.
The entry's pounds '29' and zero shillings/pence are clearly written and aligned in the columns. The name is slightly uncertain but appears to be a regular '... pro anno' entry.

Amount '50 0 0' is clearly written and column alignment is unambiguous. The place/name text is slightly abbreviated in the manuscript ('South ...'), but this is clearly an entry with full pounds only.

Pounds '6', shillings '7' and pence '5' read from the three narrow right columns. There is a small trailing flourish near the pence that slightly reduces confidence, but all digits fall within valid ranges.

rows.description: Horton pr quadriennio (note: 'cant. divid: quare whether this part ...')
rows.notes: Shillings '18' and pence '7' are aligned in the s and d columns; the pounds column shows a small 0. The descriptive text contains an extended marginal note and is partially abbreviated and cramped, reducing text confidence.
The rightmost three columns show '11 13 1' aligned opposite this entry. Ink is slightly lighter but digits are isolated and within normal ranges.
Reads as '4 3 5' in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. Handwriting of the place name is moderately ambiguous, but the numbers are aligned and valid.
This line shows a clear '10 0 0' in the columns. The surname 'Barret' is legible and the 'pr biennio' abbreviation is consistent with other rows.

The description is faint and uncertain (reads 'Calest' or 'Celest'), and the amount in the pounds column is small and faint (looks like '1 0 0'). Due to faded ink and uncertainty about the name, confidence is low.
Name and alias phrasing visible though cramped. Amount columns read '4 15 7' clearly aligned; the script for the alias reduces textual confidence slightly.

rows.description: Cudlington Mill pr quinquenio paid in part
rows.notes: Description contains a visible note 'paid in part' which is legible. The numeric columns read roughly '2 10 2' though ink blur and a small blot near the pence reduce confidence.
Numeric trio aligned as '4 15 8' opposite this entry. The place name 'Bampton' is slightly faded but readable in context of surrounding list.
'7 1 11' appears in the columns but the pence area has a dark flourish that could be a decoration; chosen reading '11d' since it fits the column and is legible on close inspection.
The pounds column reads '6' and both s/d columns are '0 0'. The name 'Clifton Dorry' is written in a flowing script that is moderately legible.
This line clearly records a larger sum '26 10 0' in the three columns. The place name is a little ambiguous but clearly a list-item; numeric alignment is strong.
Columns read '13 6 8' clearly. The place/name is written in a contracted hand; the numbers are clean and within valid ranges.
Shows '8 0 0' in the numeric columns. The heading 'Lallane pr Sequinanno' is a best-effort reading of an abbreviated Latin phrase; text confidence moderate.
Small sum '1 3 4' visible; the name is faint and could be 'Nidderham' or similar. Numeric columns are legible but description uncertain.
Right columns read '0 10 0' (10 shillings). 'Backgate' looks plausible in the left text; ink is slightly lighter but columns align well.
The manuscript on this row is partly obscured by a diagonal shadow; however, the numeric reading '20 0 0' is visible at the right edge. The name 'Hugh' is not certain due to the shadow.

The descriptive text is readable only with difficulty (shadow and ink fade). No clear numeric figures directly aligned to this row are legible; likely grouped with adjacent lines. Left amounts may be part of previous/next row.
Near-bottom entry with clear '20 0 0' in the right columns. The place name reads as 'Lardon Hall' or similar; numeric columns are distinct and valid.

Final descriptive line before the total is partly crossed/ink-blotted and the numeric entry (if any) is not clearly aligned. The phrase 'pr biennio' and 'part' are legible; no safe numeric reading possible.
Bottom-right total clearly written as '325. 19. 02.' This row provides a strong alignment reference for the pounds/shillings/pence column positions on the page.
